name: Maruts
description: The Maruts are storm deities in Hindu mythology, often depicted as the sons of Rudra (Shiva) and Prisni (the cow). They are fierce, powerful, and destructive, associated with storms, wind, lightning, and thunder. Their number varies in different accounts, ranging from 27 to 180, but they are generally considered a group of warrior gods, youthful and energetic. They are often depicted riding golden chariots pulled by ruddy horses or antelopes, wielding golden axes, bows, arrows, and thunderbolts. They are known for their loud and boisterous nature, their roars and shouts filling the sky during storms. The Maruts are considered allies of Indra, the king of the gods, and assist him in his battles against demons. While destructive in their power, they are also considered benevolent, bringing rain and fertility to the land. Their association with Rudra connects them to both destruction and healing, reflecting the dual nature of storms, which can be both devastating and life-giving. They are also associated with the elements of air and wind, representing the dynamic and unpredictable forces of nature. In some interpretations, they are seen as representing the vital energies of the universe, essential for maintaining balance and order. Their worship is less prevalent than that of other major deities, but their presence is felt in hymns and stories that celebrate the power and majesty of the natural world.
subtitle: Storm Gods, Sons of Rudra

keyMyths.title: Birth of the Maruts
keyMyths.description: Prisni, a cow, gave birth to the Maruts after Rudra touched her udder. They were born as a group of powerful, youthful warriors.

keyMyths.title: Maruts' Assistance to Indra
keyMyths.description: The Maruts frequently aid Indra in his battles against demons, providing their strength and storm-like powers to overcome his enemies.

keyMyths.title: Relationship with Rudra
keyMyths.description: As sons of Rudra, the Maruts inherit his destructive and transformative power. They are seen as extensions of his divine energy.

sources.author: Wendy Doniger
